gaps.title: Shift data model is display-strings only — no IDs, dates, or structured times
gaps.detail: Shifts are keyed by `${role}|${weekdayAbbr} ${dayOfMonth}` (e.g. 'Server|Mon 6') with time as the string '7:00 AM – 3:00 PM' and employee referenced by full-name string. There is no shift ID, no month/year (keys collide across months — 'Mon 6' recurs), no timezone, and edits address shifts by array index. ScheduleView.jsx line 6 pins ANCHOR to Jul 6 2026 so mock data only aligns with that one week; navigating to any other week shows an empty grid. Prisma schema needs Shift{id, locationId, positionId, date, startsAt, endsAt, employeeId?, status} and every component's data shape must be remapped.

gaps.title: No conflict-detection logic exists — all conflicts are hardcoded strings
gaps.detail: The 'conflict' status and prose conflictReason ('Overlaps Sam's 5–11 PM Dishwasher shift the same day') are baked into INITIAL_SHIFTS in ManagerApp.jsx (lines 32, 61). saveShift() never computes overlaps and always writes status 'draft'/'open', so editing a conflicted shift silently clears the flag and creating an overlapping shift raises nothing. The dashboard card, header ConflictChip, and cell styling all depend on this status. Overlap/availability/time-off conflict detection must be built server-side from scratch — the UI only defines how conflicts are displayed.

gaps.title: Navigation is useState tab switching, not routes
gaps.detail: ManagerApp.jsx holds `const [nav, setNav] = useState('dashboard')` and conditionally renders screens; Team and Time off screens are inline JSX in ManagerApp rather than components. No URLs, no deep links, no back button. Must be decomposed into App Router routes (/dashboard, /schedule, /team, /availability, /timeoff, /swaps) with the sidebar as a layout; dialog and view-mode state can stay client-side.

gaps.title: Entire kit is window-global IIFEs on CDN React/Babel/lucide — nothing is importable
gaps.detail: Every file is `(function(){...})()` reading React from the global scope and exporting via window.__rhManager* / window.RosterHouseDesignSystem_17c92d (from ../../_ds_bundle.js); index.html loads react, react-dom, @babel/standalone, and lucide from unpkg and transpiles JSX in the browser. None of this runs in Next.js: components must be rewritten as ES modules ('use client' where stateful), the design-system bundle replaced with real imports, lucide replaced by lucide-react (the `<i data-lucide>` + `window.lucide.createIcons()` in a dependency-array-less useEffect in ManagerApp.jsx line 93 fights React reconciliation and references window at render time), and styling depends on ../../styles.css CSS variables that must be brought into the app.

gaps.title: No auth, session, or permission model
gaps.detail: Manager 'Jamie Park' and 'Downtown location' are hardcoded; there is no login screen, no role gate, no permission-denied state on any view. The manager app mutates schedules and approves requests, so auth + manager-role authorization is a precondition for every mutation the kit implies.

gaps.title: Approve/Deny mutations are dead or undifferentiated
gaps.detail: Time-off Approve/Deny buttons (ManagerApp.jsx lines 219-220) have no onClick at all. Swap Approve and Deny both call the same decide(id) which only removes the row locally (SwapApprovals.jsx lines 42-43). The MVP loop 'employee requests swap → manager approves → shift reassigns → parties notified' has zero backing logic; approve must reassign the shift and both paths must persist a decision + notify.

gaps.title: Publish is an app-wide boolean with fake notification counts
gaps.detail: publish() flips every draft shift across ALL weeks to confirmed and sets one global published flag; the Draft/Published badge never varies by week. The confirm dialog claims '5 employees will be notified' while the success toast says '10 employees notified by push and text' — both hardcoded. Needs per-week (or per-schedule) publish state, a real recipient count, and a notification pipeline (push/SMS) that doesn't exist anywhere in the kit.

gaps.title: AssignShiftDialog has no validation and no eligibility filtering
gaps.detail: Start/End are free-text Inputs parsed by splitting on the en-dash ' – '; no time picker, no start<end or format validation, empty times save successfully. Employee dropdown is a hardcoded 10-name list with no filtering by role, availability, or time-off, and no conflict warning on save. Backend needs structured time inputs plus an eligibility/conflict check endpoint.

gaps.title: Dashboard stats are all fake and one requires missing wage data
gaps.detail: Coverage gaps '2', pending requests '3', labor cost '$4,120', and the clocked-in list are hardcoded; the conflictCount prop that IS passed in is ignored in favor of literal '2 shifts have conflicts' text. 'Projected labor cost' requires an hourly wage/rate field that exists nowhere in the design; 'Clocked in now' requires time-clock entries written by the mobile app. Each stat needs a real aggregation query.

gaps.title: Roles/positions are hardcoded and per-location config is a noop
gaps.detail: ROLES = ['Line cook','Server','Dishwasher','Host'] is a constant in WeekGrid.jsx driving the grid rows, DayView, and MonthView counts. The sidebar location switcher's onLocationClick is `() => {}`. A Position entity (per location) and a Location entity with a switcher are needed; otherwise the grid cannot render other businesses' roles.

gaps.title: Availability model is weekday booleans, inconsistent with the rest of the kit
gaps.detail: AvailabilityOverview shows only 5 of the 10 TEAM employees and reduces availability to one boolean per weekday — no time windows, no effective dates, no pending-change state (which the Team screen's AvatarStatus 'pending' implies exists). If the employee mobile app submits time-ranged availability, this view undersells the data and conflict detection can't use it as-is. Data contracts between the two apps need reconciliation.

gaps.title: No loading or error states anywhere in the kit
gaps.detail: Every screen renders synchronously from in-memory constants. Zero skeletons, spinners, fetch-error, retry, or save-failure affordances exist to copy from the design. These must be designed net-new for all six screens plus both dialogs when data becomes async.

gaps.title: Dead 'Add shift' header button
gaps.detail: The secondary 'Add shift' button in the Schedule header (ManagerApp.jsx line 171) has no onClick. Cell-level '+ Add' works, so either wire the header button to open AssignShiftDialog with role/day pickers or drop it.

gaps.title: 'Today' button navigates to the hardcoded anchor week, not today
gaps.detail: ScheduleView's Today button sets offset to 0, which is ANCHOR (Jul 6 2026), and all date math derives from that constant. Port must anchor to the location's current week (respecting week-start convention and timezone).

gaps.title: Missing empty states on Team, Time off, Availability; month view lacks conflict indicators
gaps.detail: Team, Time off, and Availability render nothing (not even a message) when their arrays are empty; only DayView, WeekGridCell, and SwapApprovals have designed empty states. MonthView badges show shift counts but hide conflicts and open shifts, weakening the drill-down cue.

gaps.title: Team management actions absent
gaps.detail: The Team screen is display-only: no invite/add employee, no role editing, no deactivation. Without an employee-creation flow somewhere, the manager cannot seed the roster that every other screen depends on. (Could live in an admin/onboarding flow, but no design exists for it.)

managerKit.notes: Navigation: single-page useState tab switching in ManagerApp.jsx ('dashboard'|'schedule'|'team'|'availability'|'timeoff'|'swaps'); Team and Time off screens are inline JSX in ManagerApp rather than separate components; ScheduleView adds its own useState for day/week/month mode and period offset. Conflict detection: purely presentational — 'conflict' statuses and reason strings are hardcoded in mock data; no computation exists, and saveShift/publish never recompute or block on conflicts (publish is allowed with conflicts present). Porting hazards: window-global IIFE module system, window.RosterHouseDesignSystem_17c92d design-system bundle (../../_ds_bundle.js) and ../../styles.css CSS-variable dependency, CDN unpkg scripts (React 18.3.1 UMD, @babel/standalone in-browser JSX, lucide UMD), <i data-lucide> icons hydrated by window.lucide.createIcons() inside a useEffect with no dependency array, toLocaleDateString('en-US') formatting, en-dash ' – ' time-string parsing, and fixed ANCHOR date of 2026-07-06 that the mock shift keys ('Mon 6'…'Sun 12') only match for that single week. Everything stateful must become 'use client' components; all mock constants (INITIAL_SHIFTS, TEAM, TIME_OFF, TEAM_AVAILABILITY, EMPLOYEES, INITIAL_REQUESTS, ROLES, clockedIn, dashboard stats) map to Prisma entities: Location, User/Employee(+wage for labor cost), Position, Shift, Availability, TimeOffRequest, SwapRequest, TimeClockEntry, plus a per-week publish/notification concept. Notification copy ('notified by push and text') implies a push+SMS pipeline with no other design support.

gaps.title: No data layer anywhere — every screen renders module-level hardcoded arrays
gaps.detail: MY_SHIFTS, OPEN_SHIFTS, NOTIFICATIONS are constants at EmployeeApp.jsx:5-20; availability, clock state, and preferences are local useState with no persistence. There is zero fetch/query/mutation plumbing, so no screen has loading or error handling to port. Every screen needs a server data contract (Prisma models: User, Location, Invite, Shift, ShiftAssignment, OpenShift, SwapRequest, TimeOffRequest, Availability, TimeEntry, Notification, NotificationPreferences) plus loading/empty/error states designed from scratch.

gaps.title: Auth is entirely fake — inputs uncontrolled, no session, no invite token
gaps.detail: Login and Accept-invite forms never read their input values; onLogin/onJoin just set useState auth='app' (EmployeeApp.jsx:475-501). No credential validation, no session/JWT, no route protection. Invite flow has no token/deep-link handling — real invites arrive as a URL (e.g. /invite/[token]) that must resolve inviter/location/role server-side; the UI hardcodes 'Jamie Park' and 'Downtown location'. MVP loop (employee logs in and sees their real shifts) cannot work.

gaps.title: All core employee mutations are dead or local-only
gaps.detail: The four actions that define the employee MVP loop do not exist as operations: (1) Request swap buttons at EmployeeApp.jsx:149 and :346 have no onClick and there is no swap flow UI at all; (2) claim open shift is a local setClaimed toggle (line 329) with no mutation or approval semantics; (3) availability has no save action — edits are lost on tab switch; (4) time-off 'Send request' only closes the dialog (line 253); (5) clock in/out is a local boolean with no timestamp, no TimeEntry write, and no geolocation despite the on-screen copy promising location verification.

gaps.title: No routing — state-machine navigation cannot port to App Router
gaps.detail: Navigation is three useState values in EmployeeApp (auth, tab, push at lines 475-478); shift detail receives a shift object by reference, not an id. Nothing maps to URLs: browser back breaks, deep links (invite links, notification tap-through, shift links) are impossible. Must be restructured to Next.js routes, e.g. /login, /invite/[token], /(app)/shifts, /(app)/shifts/[id], /(app)/availability, /(app)/clock, /(app)/open-shifts, /(app)/profile, /(app)/notifications, with the tab bar as a layout component and middleware-based auth guarding.

gaps.title: Runtime architecture is browser-global UMD + Babel-standalone — none of it ports directly
gaps.detail: index.html loads React 18 dev UMD, Babel standalone, and lucide from unpkg CDNs and pulls components from window.RosterHouseDesignSystem_17c92d (../../_ds_bundle.js). The DS components (Button, Badge, Card, Switch, Dialog, Input, Tabs, Select, Tag, ConflictChip) must be converted to importable TypeScript modules; icons use <i data-lucide> plus window.lucide.createIcons() in a dependency-less useEffect (re-runs every render, EmployeeApp.jsx:480-482) and must move to lucide-react; ../../styles.css CSS variables must be imported globally.

gaps.title: Shift status is cosmetic — Badge hardcoded 'Confirmed' everywhere
gaps.detail: MY_SHIFTS items carry a status field but HomeScreen (line 108) and ShiftDetailScreen (line 126) render a literal 'Confirmed' badge. No designs exist for pending/unpublished, swap-pending, or cancelled shift states, which the scheduling loop will produce immediately after launch.

gaps.title: No offline handling, especially for time clock
gaps.detail: This is a mobile web app for hourly workers; the time clock claims geolocation verification but has no geolocation code, no offline queue, and no failure state. A clock-in that silently no-ops offline loses payroll data. At minimum need optimistic UI + retry/queue and explicit offline/error banners.

gaps.title: No approval/pending semantics for claims, swaps, or time off
gaps.detail: Claiming an open shift flips instantly to 'Claimed'; real flow needs pending-manager-approval and already-taken conflict states. Swap requests and time-off requests have no status tracking UI (no list of my requests, no accepted/declined feedback) even though notification copy references 'Swap request approved'.

gaps.title: Notifications are read-only decoration
gaps.detail: No unread flag or bell badge, no mark-as-read mutation, no tap-through to the referenced shift/request, and the tone field in the mock data is never rendered. Also no push-notification registration despite UI copy promising push alerts for time-off decisions.

gaps.title: PhoneShell demo frame is not a real mobile layout
gaps.detail: PhoneShell (EmployeeApp.jsx:455-473) is a fixed 390x844 div with a fake bezel for the design gallery. The real app must use full viewport, responsive sizing, and safe-area insets; the bottom tab bar's padding '8px 4px 14px' is a hardcoded stand-in for env(safe-area-inset-bottom).

gaps.title: Home banner totals hardcoded and no forgot-password flow
gaps.detail: '3 shifts · 24 hrs total' (line 94) is literal text — must be computed from the user's published shifts. 'Forgot password?' (line 51) is inert text with no reset flow designed; needed soon after launch for a workforce that forgets passwords often.

gaps.title: Availability advanced hours are unvalidated free-text inputs
gaps.detail: Start/end times are plain text Inputs (lines 219-234) with placeholder-style values; needs a time picker or select with validation (end after start) before the data can be trusted by the scheduler. Also edits are lost when switching Simple/Advanced tabs since Tabs remounts content around shared state that persists, but leaving the screen entirely discards everything.

gaps.title: Time-off dialog lacks date validation
gaps.detail: No check that end >= start or that dates are set before 'Send request'; reason select has an 'other' branch with textarea but no required-field enforcement.

gaps.title: Profile has no edit affordances
gaps.detail: Name/phone/password cannot be changed; avatar initials 'MG' are hardcoded rather than derived. Logout button dead (covered under auth blocker but the button itself also needs a handler).

gaps.title: lucide createIcons runs on every render
gaps.detail: React.useEffect at EmployeeApp.jsx:480-482 has no dependency array; irrelevant after migrating to lucide-react but a perf smell if the pattern is copied.

employeeKit.notes: All 8 readme-claimed screens exist (Login, Accept invite, My Shifts home + Shift detail overlay, Availability with time-off dialog, Time Clock, Open Shifts/Swap, Notifications overlay, Profile) — 9 distinct screen components total in EmployeeApp.jsx. Coverage claim is accurate at the visual level; the gap is that essentially nothing is functional. Navigation/auth faking: EmployeeApp holds three useState values — auth ('login'|'invite'|'app'), tab (bottom-bar key), and push (overlay descriptor for shiftDetail/notifications with the shift passed by object reference). Any tap on 'Log in' or 'Join team' enters the app; credentials are never read because all form inputs are uncontrolled. What breaks on Next.js port: (1) no URLs — browser back, refresh, deep links (invite links, notification tap-through) all break; every screen needs a route and shift detail needs an id param; (2) window.RosterHouseDesignSystem_17c92d global + _ds_bundle.js + Babel-standalone JSX transform + React UMD dev builds from unpkg — all must become proper TS module imports; (3) <i data-lucide> icons + window.lucide.createIcons() in an every-render useEffect must become lucide-react components; (4) ../../styles.css CSS variables must be imported globally (inline styles reference tokens like --surface-brand, --text-h1-size heavily); (5) PhoneShell fixed 390x844 bezel is gallery chrome to delete. index.html carries a @dsCard gallery annotation (group='Employee Mobile', viewport 440x900). Useful token detail for the schema step: shift mock fields are {role, time, day, location, status, coworkers[], notes}; open shift {role, time, day}; notification {title, body, tone, when}; availability is per-weekday {available, start, end}; time-off request {startDate, endDate, reason enum vacation|sick|personal|other, note}. Note components/ itself is clean (inline SVGs only); the CDN dependency is entirely in ui_kits. Also readme.md's index references an assets/icons/ directory that does not exist in the export.

For the Prisma port this is a data-model landmine: shifts must be real timestamps with validation, formatted only at render — the string convention (en-dash with spaces) cannot round-trip safely.

For a Next.js App Router app this forces fully client-managed forms or a rewrite of the primitives to be form-native.

gaps.detail: EmployeeApp LoginScreen's 'Forgot password?' and 'New here? Accept your invite' are styled divs (one without any onClick). In the Next.js port these become next/link anchors; a styled Link/TextButton primitive would keep them consistent.

components.notes: Systemic porting facts that apply to every component: (1) All 16 primitives are plain .jsx with hand-written sibling .d.ts files; the port should merge them into typed .tsx and drop the .d.ts. The .d.ts files all return `JSX.Element`, which no longer resolves under React 19/Next 15 types (use React.JSX.Element or ReactElement). (2) Every component styles exclusively via inline style objects referencing CSS custom properties from styles.css → tokens/*.css; the app must import styles.css (or a translated tokens layer) globally in app/layout.tsx. tokens/fonts.css pulls Figtree via a Google Fonts CSS @import — replace with next/font/google to avoid render-blocking CSS import and external requests. (3) 13 of 16 components require 'use client' as written (hooks and/or handlers); only Badge, ConflictChip, and AvatarStatus are server-safe, and Card/Button/Input/WeekGridCell only need client status because hover/focus visuals are done in JS — moving those to CSS pseudo-classes would shrink the client boundary considerably. (4) No component forwards refs, spreads rest props, or accepts className; every primitive is a closed API and the UI kits already hit the limits (onClick on inner divs inside Card, style dropped by Button). (5) Accessibility is the dominant defect class: no focus styles anywhere, form controls without native inputs, divs as buttons/tabs/options, dialogs without focus traps, color-only status. Budget for an a11y pass, or map these APIs onto Radix/Base-UI equivalents while keeping the token-driven styling. (6) UI kits are IIFE scripts communicating via window globals (window.RosterHouseDesignSystem_17c92d, window.__rh*) with React 18 UMD + Babel Standalone in index.html — demo scaffolding to discard, but the screen JSX inside is a faithful spec for the App Router routes. (7) ScheduleView anchors to a hardcoded ANCHOR = new Date(2026, 6, 6) and en-US toLocaleDateString; shift/day identity is string keys like 'Server|Mon 6' — port must key by real dates/IDs. (8) Demo copy bugs to not carry over: ManagerApp publish dialog says '5 employees will be notified' while the toast says '10 employees notified'; Toast shows a checkmark for all tones.

files_read: /Users/gary/dev/RosterHouse/RosterHouse Design System/_ds_bundle.js (skimmed: header manifest + first ~120 lines)
directory listings + greps across components/, ui_kits/, guidelines/, tokens/
findings: FONTS TODAY: Single mechanism — tokens/fonts.css line 6 does `@import url("https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Figtree:wght@400;500;600;700;800&display=swap")`. Google Fonts CDN only; zero font files ship in the export (manifest `fonts: []`, `brandFonts: [{family: Figtree, status: ok, tokens: [--font-sans]}]`). Figtree is an explicit SUBSTITUTION — readme states no brand typeface was provided and Figtree was chosen as 'nearest match'. Font stack token: `--font-sans: "Figtree", "Segoe UI", system-ui, -apple-system, sans-serif` (tokens/typography.css line 6); `--font-mono` is pure system stack (no webfont).

ICONS TODAY: Two distinct mechanisms. (a) UI kits and guideline cards load Lucide via UMD CDN `<script src="https://unpkg.com/lucide@latest/dist/umd/lucide.js">` (UNPINNED @latest, no SRI integrity attribute — the react/babel scripts DO have integrity hashes) and render icons imperatively with `<i data-lucide="name">` + `window.lucide.createIcons()` called after mount (e.g. EmployeeApp.jsx line 481). This pattern is fundamentally incompatible with React re-renders — icons vanish on state updates unless createIcons is re-run. (b) The 16 reusable DS components mostly embed hand-copied Lucide SVG paths INLINE (ConflictChip.jsx, Toast.jsx, Checkbox.jsx, ShiftBlock.jsx contain raw `<svg stroke="currentColor" strokeWidth=2.2/2.5>` markup), so the core component library has NO runtime icon dependency. Icon vocabulary used across kit: calendar, clock, bell, user, users, settings, home, plus, check, x, chevron-right/left, alert-triangle, repeat, map-pin, phone, search, play, square (per guidelines/iconography.html + ui_kit greps). Stroke convention 1.5–2px.

DEMO-ONLY CDN SCAFFOLDING (do not port): ui_kits/*/index.html and components/*.card.html load react@18.3.1 + react-dom@18.3.1 development UMD builds and @babel/standalone@7.29.0 from unpkg, and execute .jsx via `<script type="text/babel">`. _ds_bundle.js is a prebuilt IIFE bundle of all 16 components compiled to React.createElement, registered on `window.RosterHouseDesignSystem_17c92d` with per-file try/catch error capture and source hashes. All of this is preview harness, not production code.

TOKEN CSS STRUCTURE: styles.css is a 5-line pure @import aggregator (fonts.css → colors.css → typography.css → spacing.css → effects.css, in that order; manifest `globalCssPaths` confirms the same order). Every token is a CSS custom property on a single `:root` block — no classes, no resets, no element styles anywhere in the token layer. colors.css layers semantic aliases (--surface-*, --text-*, --border-*, --accent-*, --status-*) over raw scales (--green-*, --amber-*, --neutral-*, --red-*, --blue-*) via var() indirection. 137 tokens total per manifest. All type/spacing sizes are px (not rem). NO dark mode: manifest `themes: []`, zero hits for prefers-color-scheme/data-theme/color-scheme across the entire export — single light 'warm paper' theme only. The alias indirection layer means a dark theme could later be added by re-pointing aliases in a `[data-theme="dark"]` block without touching components, but nothing exists today.

MANIFEST (_ds_manifest.json): namespace `RosterHouseDesignSystem_17c92d`; 16 components with sourcePaths (containers: Card/Dialog/Tabs; feedback: Badge/Tag/Toast/Tooltip; forms: Button/Checkbox/Input/Select/Switch; scheduling: AvatarStatus/ConflictChip/ShiftBlock/WeekGridCell); 20 preview cards; full token inventory with kind classification (color/font/spacing/radius/shadow/other); `startingPoints: []`, `templates: []`, `themes: []`, `source: "spa"`. Metadata bug: the six --text-* COLOR aliases (--text-primary, --text-secondary, etc.) are misclassified as `kind: "font"` in both the manifest and the oxlint x-omelette block.

SKILL.md: minimal 9-line Claude-skill wrapper (name: rosterhouse-design, user-invocable) that just says 'read README.md, copy assets, follow the rules for production code'. All real conventions live in readme.md: content voice (sentence case everywhere, never title case, no emoji in UI chrome, 12-hour clock times '7:00 AM–3:00 PM' never military, durations as '8 hrs' not decimal, specific non-blaming error copy), interaction rules (hover = darken one step via --accent-hover, press = darken further + translateY(1px), no scale/shrink, 120–280ms ease-out only, no bounce/spring/confetti), visual rules (no gradients/photography/textures, warm-tinted shadows never pure black, no transparency/blur except modal scrim rgba(10,20,17,0.4), initials-on-color avatars, radius mapping: 6px small controls / 10px inputs+buttons / 16px cards / 22px sheets / pill badges+FAB), layout rules (manager web: fixed left rail + sticky week-grid header; employee mobile: single-column, bottom tab bar / bottom sheet for primary actions). These are app-level conventions the Next.js build should preserve verbatim.

ADHERENCE TOOLING (_adherence.oxlintrc.json): oxlint config, all rules at 'warn'. Three enforcement categories: (1) no-restricted-syntax regex literals banning raw hex colors ('use a design-system color token via var()'), raw px values, and any font-family other than Figtree; (2) AST-selector prop contracts for all 16 components — allowed prop lists plus enum validation (Button variant: primary|secondary|ghost|accent|danger, size sm|md|lg; Badge tone: success|warning|danger|info|neutral; ShiftBlock status: confirmed|open|conflict|draft; AvatarStatus status: available|unavailable|pending|off; Toast tone; Tag color: neutral|brand|accent; Tooltip side: top|bottom); (3) no-restricted-imports forcing imports through 'index.js' barrel — BUT no index.js exists anywhere in the export (find returned zero), so that rule references a nonexistent file. Also an `x-omelette` metadata block re-listing components (all `replaces: []`), full token list, tokenKinds, and fontFamilies: [Figtree]. The prop-enum contracts are the durable value — they translate directly into TypeScript union types for the ported components (the .d.ts files already exist alongside each .jsx).

COMPONENT FORMAT: each component ships as .jsx (import React from 'react', named export, all styling via inline style objects referencing var(--token)), plus a .d.ts and a .prompt.md. Hover/press states are implemented with React useState + onMouseEnter/onMouseLeave (e.g. Button.jsx, Card.jsx) rather than CSS pseudo-classes — this breaks on touch devices (the primary employee-mobile audience) and should be reimplemented as CSS classes when porting. Button.jsx danger variant hardcodes fg '#fff' (lines 48–50), violating the export's own raw-hex adherence rule; readme also mandates 'nothing is pure white/black' so danger fg should arguably be --neutral-50.

assets/icons/ VERDICT: does NOT exist. readme.md line 42 ('Index' section) claims '`assets/icons/` — Lucide icon set (SVG)', but `ls` confirms no assets/ directory at all, and readme's own Iconography section (lines 138–142) contradicts the index: 'no binary asset download was possible from this environment, so nothing is duplicated into assets/ — components reference the CDN directly.' The only binary asset in the export is uploads/pasted-1783291965045-0.png (26KB).

TYPOGRAPHY DETAIL: token scale covers display-xl/lg/md (56/44/34px w700), h1/h2/h3 (28/22/18px, 700/600/600), body-lg/body/body-sm (17/15/13px w400), caption (12px w600 +0.02em tracking), label (13px w600). Note: fonts.css loads weight 800 and readme says '700/800 for headlines', but no typography token actually uses 800 — keep 800 in the next/font weights array anyway since guideline cards reference it ('Figtree 700–800').

gaps.title: All fonts and icons are runtime CDN dependencies
gaps.detail: Google Fonts CDN @import for Figtree (tokens/fonts.css) and unpkg Lucide UMD for icons must be replaced before production. fonts.css must be dropped entirely in favor of next/font/google (Figtree, weights 400/500/600/700/800, variable: '--font-sans') so the token file's --font-sans keeps resolving; icons must move to the lucide-react npm package. The `lucide@latest` script is also unpinned with no SRI hash — a supply-chain risk even for previews.

gaps.title: readme-advertised assets/icons/ directory does not exist
gaps.detail: readme.md line 42 lists `assets/icons/ — Lucide icon set (SVG)` but no assets/ directory exists in the export; readme's Iconography section admits nothing was downloaded. No local icon files ship at all. Consequence: the Next.js app cannot copy icons from the export — it must add lucide-react as a dependency (or vendor SVGs) and the readme index should be corrected.

gaps.title: No dark mode / no alternate themes
gaps.detail: Manifest `themes: []`; zero prefers-color-scheme, data-theme, or color-scheme usage anywhere. Only a single light 'warm paper' theme exists. For an employee mobile web app used for night-shift clock-ins this is a real product gap. Mitigation path exists: the semantic alias layer in colors.css (--surface-*/--text-*/--border-*/--accent-*/--status-*) means dark mode can be added later as a `[data-theme="dark"]` override block without touching components — but every alias value would need designing from scratch.

gaps.title: Adherence config references a nonexistent index.js barrel
gaps.detail: _adherence.oxlintrc.json's no-restricted-imports rule requires importing DS components 'from index.js, not component internals', and its overrides exempt **/index.js — but no index.js exists anywhere in the export. Carrying the config over verbatim enforces an impossible convention. The port should create the barrel (e.g. components/ui/index.ts) and rewrite the restricted-import globs to the app's actual paths.

gaps.title: Icon rendering in UI kits is imperative and React-unsafe
gaps.detail: UI kit screens use `<i data-lucide="...">` + a one-shot window.lucide.createIcons() after mount (EmployeeApp.jsx line 481). Any React re-render that adds/changes an `<i data-lucide>` node produces an empty element. All ui_kit screen code being used as reference for the Next.js pages must have icon usage rewritten to lucide-react components — it cannot be copied as-is.

gaps.title: Components are .jsx with useState-driven hover/press, not production-ready TSX
gaps.detail: All 16 components use inline style objects plus React state for hover/hover-active (onMouseEnter/onMouseDown), which produces no hover fallback on touch devices and defeats SSR/CSS optimization. .d.ts files exist and encode the prop contracts, so porting to typed .tsx with CSS-class states (CSS Modules or Tailwind arbitrary values on the tokens) is mechanical but required — treat export components as specs, not drop-in code.

gaps.title: Export's own code violates its adherence rules
gaps.detail: Button.jsx danger variant hardcodes '#fff' (raw hex, and 'pure white' which readme forbids); many components hardcode raw px literals (Button padding '6px 12px', fontSize 13/14/16; Dialog width 420) that the raw-px lint rule flags. The px rule as written (Literal regex \b\d+px\b) will warn on legitimate one-off dimensions too — carry the hex-color and font-family rules over as-is, but expect to loosen or scope the px rule.

gaps.title: Manifest token-kind misclassification
gaps.detail: --text-primary/-secondary/-tertiary/-inverse/-brand/-link are colors but tagged kind:'font' in _ds_manifest.json and the oxlint x-omelette block. Harmless at runtime; only matters if tooling is built on the manifest's kind field.

gaps.title: px-based type and spacing tokens (no rem)
gaps.detail: All font-size/spacing tokens are absolute px, so browser/user font-size scaling is ignored — an accessibility consideration for the hourly-worker mobile audience. Converting token values to rem at port time (keeping the same visual sizes) is cheap now and expensive later.

gaps.title: Whole system is an unvalidated from-scratch proposal
gaps.detail: readme Caveats: no Figma, no logo, no brand fonts, no existing identity — palette/type/components are a proposal from a spec + Q&A. No logo exists (wordmark is plain Figtree text). Phase-3 surfaces (payroll, reporting, tip pooling), multi-location switching, onboarding wizard, and billing screens have no designs. Not a porting defect, but the synthesis step should treat visuals as v1-replaceable and keep tokens as the single source of truth.

external_deps: https://unpkg.com/lucide@latest/dist/umd/lucide.js (ui_kits/*/index.html, components/*.card.html, guidelines/iconography.html — unpinned, no SRI; replace with lucide-react npm package)
https://unpkg.com/react@18.3.1/umd/react.development.js (demo shells only — superseded by Next.js React)
https://unpkg.com/@babel/standalone@7.29.0/babel.min.js (demo shells only — in-browser JSX transform, drop entirely)
porting_plan_notes: globals.css strategy: copy tokens/colors.css + typography.css + spacing.css + effects.css verbatim into app/styles/tokens/ (or merge their :root blocks into one tokens.css) and @import them at the top of app/globals.css; do NOT copy fonts.css or styles.css. Preserve manifest globalCssPaths order (colors → typography → spacing → effects). Add `body { background: var(--surface-page); color: var(--text-primary); font-family: var(--font-sans); }` since the export's token layer intentionally contains no element styles.

next/font wiring: `const figtree = Figtree({ subsets: ['latin'], weight: ['400','500','600','700','800'], variable: '--font-figtree' })` on <html className={figtree.variable}>, then change the --font-sans token to `var(--font-figtree), "Segoe UI", system-ui, -apple-system, sans-serif` — one-line token edit keeps every component/token reference working with zero CDN traffic.

Icons: install lucide-react pinned; build a thin Icon wrapper defaulting strokeWidth 1.75–2 and size 16–26 to match the kit's 1.5–2px stroke convention; the needed set is ~18 icons (calendar, clock, bell, user, users, settings, home, plus, check, x, chevron-right, chevron-left, alert-triangle, repeat, map-pin, phone, search, play, square). Components with inline SVG (ConflictChip, Toast, Checkbox, ShiftBlock) can keep inline paths or switch to lucide-react — either is CDN-free.

Rebuild the 16 components as typed .tsx using the shipped .d.ts prop contracts and the oxlint enum lists as TS unions (Button variant/size, Badge tone, ShiftBlock status, AvatarStatus status, Tag color, Toast tone, Tooltip side); replace useState hover/press with CSS :hover/:active classes per readme interaction rules (darken one step / translateY(1px)). Export everything from a single barrel to satisfy the adherence convention.

Carry over adherence linting adapted: keep raw-hex-color ban and Figtree-only font-family rule (oxlint or ESLint no-restricted-syntax on app source, excluding the token files themselves); recreate the restricted-imports rule against the new barrel path; expect to scope or drop the raw-px rule; alternatively encode enums in TS and let the compiler replace the JSX prop-contract selectors.

Preserve readme conventions as app copy/UX rules: sentence case everywhere, 12-hour times, '8 hrs' durations, no emoji in chrome, calm confirmations, specific error copy ('This shift overlaps with Maria's 2–6 PM shift'), manager left-rail + sticky week-grid header, employee bottom tab bar. Consider converting px tokens to rem during the copy step.

Do not port: styles.css (trivial aggregator), fonts.css, _ds_bundle.js, *.card.html, ui_kits/*/index.html shells, Babel/React UMD scripts. ui_kits/*.jsx screens are reference material for page layouts only — their data-lucide usage and window.__rh_* globals must not survive the port.

missing_screens: Manager login (no auth screens exist anywhere in ui_kits/manager-web/ — ManagerApp.jsx boots straight to dashboard)
Manager signup / account creation
Manager forgot-password / reset-password
Org & location onboarding wizard (create company, create location, define roles) — readme.md line 158-160 confirms intentionally not built, but the product cannot bootstrap without it
Location switcher / location management (Sidebar location control is wired to a no-op: onLocationClick={() => {}} in ManagerApp.jsx line 156)

Invite employee / add team member (manager side of the invite that employee-mobile's AcceptInviteScreen presupposes — Team view in ManagerApp.jsx lines 194-206 is a read-only list with no add/invite affordance)
Team member detail / edit (contact info, role assignment, wage for the 'Projected labor cost' stat, deactivate/remove)
Roles/positions management (ROLES is hardcoded to ['Line cook','Server','Dishwasher','Host'] in WeekGrid.jsx line 4 and the week grid is structured as role rows — no screen to create/rename roles)
Manager settings (org settings, schedule/week-start settings, notification preferences — employee has notification prefs in ProfileScreen, manager has none)
Manager account menu / profile / logout (sidebar avatar 'Jamie Park' in Sidebar.jsx lines 71-88 is not interactive; there is no logout on manager web)
Timesheets / clock-in review for manager (dashboard shows 'Clocked in now' and 'Projected labor cost' but there is no screen to view, edit, approve, or export punch data)
Time-off request history / decided-requests view for manager (ManagerApp timeoff view lists only 2 pending items)
Error pages for the web app (404, 500, access-denied) and loading/skeleton states — none exist in either kit
Billing / subscription screens (readme line 160 flags as not built)
Employee forgot-password / reset-password ('Forgot password?' in EmployeeApp.jsx lines 50-52 is static text with no onClick and no target screen)

Employee invite entry / invite-link landing (AcceptInviteScreen at EmployeeApp.jsx lines 63-78 assumes the invite context — 'Jamie Park invited you to join Downtown location' — with no code/token entry or link-landing screen)

Swap request composer (choose which shift, choose a coworker or 'anyone qualified', optional note, confirm) — both 'Request swap' buttons (ShiftDetailScreen line 149, SwapScreen line 346) have no onClick and no destination

Incoming swap offers screen for teammates (a coworker must be able to see and accept/decline a swap offer before manager approval; SwapApprovals.jsx demo data implies this stage exists but no employee screen covers it)
My requests / request-status screen for employee (pending/approved/denied state for time-off requests, swap requests, and open-shift claims — currently only terminal notification cards exist)
Employee timesheet / hours history (TimeClockScreen is a stateless toggle; no past punches, weekly totals, or post-clock-out summary)
Employee edit profile / change password / phone number verification (ProfileScreen is read-only apart from notification switches)
Push-notification and location permission priming screens (product depends on push and geolocated clock-in per TimeClockScreen line 407, but there is no permission-request UX)
Employee full-schedule / calendar view (HomeScreen shows only a 3-item upcoming list; no week view, no past shifts, no view of the published week as a whole)
missing_flows: Manager first-run: sign up → create org → create location → define roles → invite staff. Zero screens exist for any step; the manager web app starts logged-in with seeded data.

Employee onboarding handoff: manager sends invite → employee receives link/text → lands on Accept invite. The manager-side invite creation and the employee-side link/code landing are both missing; only the middle screen (AcceptInviteScreen) exists, so the journey cannot start.

MVP loop step 2 (build with conflict warnings): conflicts exist only as pre-seeded demo data (status:'conflict' in INITIAL_SHIFTS, ManagerApp.jsx lines 32, 61). saveShift (lines 108-126) always writes status 'draft' or 'open' and never evaluates availability/overlap, and AssignShiftDialog shows a ConflictChip only for already-conflicted existing shifts (AssignShiftDialog.jsx lines 47-49). There is no designed moment where assigning an unavailable or double-booked employee produces a warning — the core MVP promise has no interaction design.

MVP loop step 2 (drag): readme promises 'manager drags shifts onto a week grid' but no drag-and-drop affordance exists anywhere; the only mechanism is click-cell → dialog. The interaction model is unresolved.

MVP loop steps 4-5 (publish → push/text → employee views): publish shows a toast, but there are no push/SMS message content designs, employee notifications are not tappable (no deep link from 'Schedule published' to the schedule), and the employee has no full-week schedule view to land on. The cross-app handoff is asserted in copy but not designed.

Phase-2 swap, end to end: employee taps 'Request swap' → DEAD END (no onClick, no composer) → teammate accept stage has no screen → manager approval exists (SwapApprovals.jsx) but Approve and Deny call the same decide() and just remove the row → resulting schedule change and employee notification are not shown. Only 1 of ~5 stages is designed.

Phase-2 open shifts, employee side of the loop: manager creates open shift (assign dialog with no employee) and approves claims — both exist — but after the employee taps 'Claim' (SwapScreen lines 329-331) the state is local-only ('Claimed'), with no pending-approval status, no approved/denied outcome surface, and no design for a denied claim.

Phase-2 time off, decision and downstream: employee request dialog exists, but manager Approve/Deny buttons in ManagerApp.jsx lines 219-221 have no onClick (no confirmation, no removal, no denial reason), the employee has no status view for a submitted request, and approved time off never surfaces in the schedule builder or AssignShiftDialog (no 'time off' conflict/indicator when scheduling Sam Torres during his approved Jul 14-16 trip).

Phase-2 time clock, manager side: employee can toggle clock in/out, but punches go nowhere — no manager timesheet review/edit/approve flow, so the time-clock feature cannot complete its purpose (payable hours).

Employee availability → manager scheduling handoff is lossy: AvailabilityScreen 'Advanced' mode captures per-day hour windows (EmployeeApp.jsx lines 212-240) but AvailabilityOverview renders only boolean day cells (AvailabilityOverview.jsx lines 29-46), and availability is not surfaced at all inside AssignShiftDialog's employee select. Also the employee screen has no Save/submit action or confirmation, so step 1 of the MVP loop has no completion state.
Forgot password on either app: no reset-request, email/SMS, or new-password screens; the employee link is dead text and the manager app has no login at all.
Add shift without a cell: the header 'Add shift' button (ManagerApp.jsx line 171) has no onClick and no variant of AssignShiftDialog with role/day pickers exists.
Multi-location: sidebar location control is a no-op and no picker/management screen exists, so a manager with two locations cannot switch or scope the schedule.
Manager logout / session end: no affordance exists on the web app.

gaps.title: No manager authentication or tenant bootstrap
gaps.detail: ui_kits/manager-web/ contains no login, signup, or org/location creation. Combined with the missing invite-employee flow, neither user population can actually get into the product. Next.js implementation will need these screens designed or improvised from the form primitives.

gaps.title: Team management is read-only
gaps.detail: ManagerApp.jsx 'team' view (lines 194-206) renders name/role/status cards only. No invite, add, edit, role-assign, wage, or deactivate. The employee app's AcceptInviteScreen references an invite that no manager screen can send.

gaps.title: Conflict detection has no trigger interaction
gaps.detail: Conflicts are seeded demo data; saveShift never computes them and AssignShiftDialog neither checks availability nor warns on overlap when assigning. The MVP's central differentiator ('conflict warnings' per readme lines 5-6) has display components (ConflictChip, ShiftBlock conflict state, WeekGridCell dashed border) but no designed moment of appearance. Needs design for: warning inside AssignShiftDialog on selecting an unavailable/double-booked employee, and availability/time-off/overtime indicators in the employee Select.

gaps.title: Swap creation entirely missing
gaps.detail: Both employee 'Request swap' buttons are inert (EmployeeApp.jsx lines 149 and 346). Phase-2's flagship feature cannot begin, making manager-side SwapApprovals unreachable in practice.

gaps.title: Drag-and-drop vs click-to-assign unresolved
gaps.detail: readme.md line 5 promises drag; every screen implements click→dialog. Either DnD interaction design (drag sources, drop targets, ghost states, mobile fallback) is missing, or the readme overstates and the click model should be canonized. Affects the core scheduling data model and component APIs.

gaps.title: Publish model is global and inconsistent
gaps.detail: publish() in ManagerApp.jsx (lines 140-152) flips ALL drafts across ALL weeks; the Draft/Published badge is a single boolean while ScheduleView navigates arbitrary weeks. No per-week publish state, no republish-after-edit flow, no unpublish, no publish history. Copy inconsistency: confirm dialog says '5 employees will be notified' (line 256), success toast says '10 employees notified' (line 150).

gaps.title: Free-text time entry with no validation states
gaps.detail: AssignShiftDialog start/end are bare Inputs with placeholder '7:00 AM' (lines 57-64); AvailabilityScreen advanced hours are the same. No time-picker component, no invalid-format error state, no end-before-start handling. This is the primary data-entry surface of the whole product.

gaps.title: Header 'Add shift' button is dead
gaps.detail: ManagerApp.jsx line 171: <Button variant="secondary">Add shift</Button> has no onClick and no dialog variant with role/day pickers. Only the per-cell '+' path is designed.

gaps.title: Time-off approve/deny is non-functional and has no downstream design
gaps.detail: ManagerApp.jsx lines 219-221: Deny/Approve have no handlers. No confirmation, no decided-state, no denial reason, no notification design, and approved time off is never reflected in the schedule grid, availability overview, or assign dialog.

gaps.title: Approve vs Deny indistinguishable in SwapApprovals
gaps.detail: SwapApprovals.jsx line 13-15: decide(id) removes the row for both actions. No differentiated outcome, no denial reason, no resulting schedule mutation, no notification to the requester or the claimant.

gaps.title: No notification content designs for push/SMS
gaps.detail: The MVP loop's 'pushed/texted' step has zero artifacts: no SMS copy templates, no push payload/preview designs, no phone-number verification step (phone is collected at invite, EmployeeApp.jsx line 71, but never verified — SMS delivery depends on it). In-app NOTIFICATIONS list is canned and cards are not tappable (no deep links, no read/unread, no bell badge count).

gaps.title: Employee availability has no save/submit affordance
gaps.detail: AvailabilityScreen (EmployeeApp.jsx lines 160-311) mutates local state with no Save button, no auto-save indicator, and no confirmation, so MVP-loop step 1 has no completion state. Also no effective-date concept (changes mid-published-week).

gaps.title: Manager availability overview is lossy and unlinked
gaps.detail: AvailabilityOverview.jsx shows boolean day cells only — the hour windows employees can set in 'Advanced' mode are dropped — and lists 5 hardcoded people vs the 10-person TEAM. No week context, no link into the schedule builder, and availability is absent from AssignShiftDialog where it is actually needed.

gaps.title: Time clock lacks everything around the toggle
gaps.detail: TimeClockScreen (EmployeeApp.jsx lines 373-412): no association with the scheduled shift, no early/late clock-in rules, no elapsed timer, no breaks, no geofence-failure or location-permission-denied state (copy on line 407 promises location verification), no post-clock-out hours summary, and no persistence surface (timesheet) on either app.

gaps.title: Wage/labor-cost data has no input surface
gaps.detail: Dashboard 'Projected labor cost $4,120' (DashboardScreen.jsx line 30) implies per-employee wages and hour math, but no screen collects wages. Either design wage entry in team-member edit or drop the stat from MVP.

gaps.title: Empty, loading, and error states almost entirely missing
gaps.detail: Only SwapApprovals ('All caught up') and DayView ('No shifts scheduled') have empty states. Missing: Team empty, Time off empty, Notifications empty, employee Home with no shifts, Open shifts empty, dashboard all-clear, failed save, offline (critical for mobile clock-in), and any loading skeletons. No 404/500 pages for the web app.

gaps.title: Manager web has no responsive design
gaps.detail: Fixed 232px sidebar (Sidebar.jsx line 15), 1440x860 target viewport (index.html dsCard), 110px+7x128px grid columns. Managers of hourly teams frequently manage from phones; there is no tablet/mobile layout or breakpoint guidance.

gaps.title: Open-shift claim feedback is misleading
gaps.detail: SwapScreen 'Claim' flips to a disabled 'Claimed' button locally (EmployeeApp.jsx lines 329-331), implying success, but SwapApprovals copy says claims are 'Awaiting your approval'. Pending/approved/denied claim states need design on the employee side.

gaps.title: Icons and fonts are CDN-only
gaps.detail: readme index lists 'assets/icons/' but no assets/ directory exists in the export (verified via file listing); Lucide loads from unpkg and Figtree from Google Fonts. For the Next.js production app these must be self-hosted (bundle lucide-react, next/font) — also a CSP and offline concern. readme lines 135-142 and 153-155 acknowledge the substitution.

gaps.title: Shift status vocabulary incomplete
gaps.detail: ShiftBlock.jsx supports confirmed/open/conflict/draft only. No visual state for swap-pending, claim-pending, covered-by-someone-else, or shift-during-approved-time-off, all of which phase-2 flows produce.

gaps.title: Team status dots lack legend and semantics
gaps.detail: AvatarStatus statuses (available/unavailable/pending/off) render as colored dots in the Team list with no legend; 'pending' presumably means invite-pending but that lifecycle is undefined anywhere.

gaps.title: Demo date model leaks into design
gaps.detail: ScheduleView.jsx anchors to hardcoded ANCHOR = July 6 2026 and keys shifts by 'Mon 6' strings (dayKeyOf), which collide across months in MonthView counts. Harmless as a mock, but signals that real date/timezone modeling is undesigned (week-start config, DST, multi-week data).

gaps.title: Publish confirmation is toast-only
gaps.detail: The 3.5s auto-dismiss toast (ManagerApp.jsx lines 150-151) is the only record of publishing; no publish history/audit or 'last published' timestamp on the schedule header.

gaps.title: Employee login is password-only despite phone-first field
gaps.detail: LoginScreen label is 'Phone or email' but the only path is password. Hourly, SMS-first workforce suggests an OTP/magic-link option; at minimum the phone-login behavior is undefined.

gaps.title: Accessibility: interactive divs throughout
gaps.detail: Sidebar nav items, mobile tab bar, clock in/out circle, week-grid '+ Add' targets, and dashboard cards are onClick divs, not buttons — no keyboard/focus semantics. The kit's focus-ring tokens exist but screens do not use focusable elements. An implementation-time fix, but worth flagging so the wiring pass adds proper semantics.

gaps.title: No legal/compliance touchpoints
gaps.detail: AcceptInviteScreen collects name/phone/password with no terms-of-service, privacy policy, or SMS-consent language (SMS consent is legally required for texting schedules in the US under TCPA).

gaps.title: No location detail for employees
gaps.detail: ShiftDetailScreen shows the location name only; no address or map link for employees who work multiple sites.

gapAnalysis.notes: Verified screen inventory (all claims checked in JSX, not the readme). Manager web ("/Users/gary/dev/RosterHouse/RosterHouse Design System/ui_kits/manager-web/"): ManagerApp.jsx shell with Dashboard (DashboardScreen.jsx), Schedule (ScheduleView.jsx day/week/month + WeekGrid.jsx + AssignShiftDialog.jsx + publish confirm dialog + success toast), Team (inline, read-only), Availability (AvailabilityOverview.jsx, read-only), Time off (inline, 2 pending cards, inert buttons), Swaps & open shifts (SwapApprovals.jsx). Employee mobile ("/Users/gary/dev/RosterHouse/RosterHouse Design System/ui_kits/employee-mobile/EmployeeApp.jsx"): Login, Accept invite, Home/My shifts, Shift detail, Availability (simple/advanced + time-off request dialog), Time clock, Open shifts/Swap, Notifications, Profile (notification prefs + logout). The readme's screen list is accurate about what exists but overstates completeness: 'drags shifts' (no DnD), 'conflict warnings' (display-only, never triggered), 'swap requests' (button exists, flow does not). readme.md lines 158-160 already self-report the biggest structural absences (onboarding wizard, multi-location, billing, phase-3 surfaces); this analysis confirms them plus the manager-auth, invite-send, swap-composer, and timesheet holes it does not mention. Severity philosophy used: blocker = an MVP-loop or phase-2 feature cannot function or users cannot enter the system; major = a designed flow dead-ends, misleads, or requires inventing significant UI at implementation time; minor = polish, semantics, or production-hardening. For the Next.js wiring step, the four blockers (auth+bootstrap, team management/invites, conflict-trigger design, swap composer) require net-new screen design before implementation can proceed; everything else can be built from existing primitives (Dialog, Select, Input, Tabs, Card, Badge) with design decisions made inline.

dataModel.notes:
MULTI-TENANCY: Two-level tenancy Organization → Location. Every domain row carries organizationId (directly or via Location); Positions, Schedules, Shifts, Availability, and all request queues are Location-scoped. The manager Sidebar renders a clickable location pill ('Downtown location' + chevron), so model users as having multiple location memberships (EmployeeProfile per location) even though the switcher UI is a stub — readme explicitly flags multi-location switching and org/location onboarding as not-yet-built screens. Enforce tenancy in every API handler (session → org → location membership check), not just in the UI.

TIMEZONES: Store an IANA timezone on Location (the single source of truth for wall-clock rendering). Shifts should persist UTC instants (startsAt/endsAt) PLUS a location-local service `date`, because (a) the grid is keyed by local day ('Mon 6'), (b) demo data includes shifts crossing midnight ('5:00 PM – 12:00 AM', '6:00 PM – 12:00 AM') which belong to the starting day's column, and (c) the readme mandates 12-hour clock display ('7:00 AM–3:00 PM', never military time) and spelled-out durations ('8 hrs', '24 hrs total'). Week starts Monday (grid and month view are Monday-first). Time inputs in the kit are free-text 12-hour strings — the API should accept/normalize 'h:mm AM/PM'.

DERIVED-NOT-STORED: (1) Shift 'conflict' status and conflictReason are computed server-side per readme/ConflictChip semantics — double-booked (overlapping shifts same employee same day, evidenced by 'Overlaps Sam's 2–6 PM Server shift the same day'), outside availability, and overtime. Do not persist a conflict flag; recompute on schedule reads and shift writes (hence the /api/shifts/validate dry-run for the dialog). (2) 'Open shift' = Shift.employeeProfileId IS NULL (AssignShiftDialog's employee placeholder is literally 'Open shift (unassigned)'). (3) UI ShiftBlock status mapping: draft = assigned + schedule/shift unpublished; confirmed = published + assigned; open = null assignee; conflict = computed overlay. (4) Employee coworkers list on Shift Detail = other assigned shifts overlapping the same location/date. (5) Dashboard stats (coverage gaps, projected labor cost, pending counts, clocked-in-now) are aggregates; labor cost requires an hourlyRate field on EmployeeProfile that no screen edits — flag as a needed settings/team-edit surface. (6) AvatarStatus dot (available/unavailable/pending/off) is derived from availability rules + approved time off + invite status.

PUBLISH FLOW: Publishing is the key transactional write — flip Schedule.status and all draft shifts to published atomically, then fan out Notification rows + push/SMS sends to distinct affected employees, returning the notified count for the toast. Notification copy differs by channel prefs (Profile switches: push/SMS on by default, email off).

EVIDENCE GAPS / INTENTIONAL OMISSIONS: No screens exist for org/location onboarding, billing, manager settings, position CRUD, employee time-off history, or reporting (readme lists these as phase-3/not built) — endpoints above cover only what the screens need plus minimal implied auth/invite plumbing. SwapRequest peer-acceptance (a teammate volunteering before manager approval) is implied by 'Sam Torres will cover your Fri shift' but has no screen; modeled as nullable coveringEmployeeProfileId settable before approval. Auth screens exist only in the employee kit; the manager web app needs the same login (role='manager') — no separate manager signup screen exists.

missed_gaps.title: Employees work multiple positions in the seed data but no qualification/eligibility model exists
missed_gaps.detail: The data model gives EmployeeProfile a single required positionId ('primary role shown everywhere'), but the manager kit's own demo data contradicts it: Sam Torres (Server in TEAM) is assigned a Dishwasher shift (ManagerApp.jsx line 61, 'Dishwasher|Wed 8'), and Alex Kim (Host in TEAM) works a Dishwasher shift (line 65) and claims a Dishwasher open shift (SwapApprovals.jsx line 7). Swap copy says 'Open to anyone qualified' and readme promises conflict warnings — both require a many-to-many Employee↔Position qualification model (e.g. EmployeePosition join table) plus eligibility filtering in AssignShiftDialog and open-shift claiming. No agent modeled cross-position work or flagged that the single-position schema cannot represent the export's own evidence.

missed_gaps.title: 'Coverage gaps' metric has no definition and no data model behind it
missed_gaps.detail: The dashboard stat 'Coverage gaps this week: 2' (DashboardScreen.jsx line 28) and the dashboard endpoint the data modeler proposed both assume 'coverage gap' is computable, but nothing defines it. Computing coverage gaps requires per-position/per-day-part staffing targets (e.g. '4 Servers on Friday dinner'), and no entity, screen, or settings surface anywhere lets a manager set required staffing levels. Either a StaffingRequirement entity + management UI must be designed, or the product must explicitly redefine the stat as 'count of open (unassigned) shifts' — a decision nobody surfaced. The gap analyst caught the analogous problem for wage/labor-cost but missed this one.

missed_gaps.title: Web push on iOS requires an installed PWA + service worker — no PWA plan exists
missed_gaps.detail: The employee app is a mobile web app whose core loop depends on push notifications ('notified by push and text', 'You'll get a push notification once it's approved or denied', push toggle in Profile). On iOS Safari, Web Push only works for PWAs added to the home screen (manifest + service worker + user install), and there is no PWA manifest, service-worker strategy, install-prompt UX, or notification-permission priming anywhere in the export or in any agent's findings. The gap analyst flagged missing 'push registration' UX and the data modeler added a PushDevice entity, but nobody flagged the platform constraint that makes push undeliverable to iPhone users without PWA installation — which for an hourly-workforce audience likely makes SMS the primary channel and materially affects the Next.js architecture (service worker, offline caching) and the notification pipeline design.

missed_gaps.title: Location has no coordinates or geofence radius despite promised on-site clock-in verification
missed_gaps.detail: TimeClockScreen copy promises 'Uses your phone's location to confirm you're on-site' (EmployeeApp.jsx line 407), and the data modeler's /api/time-clock/clock-in endpoint says 'server verifies geolocation against Location' — but the proposed Location entity has only name/address/timezone, no lat/lng and no geofence radius, so there is nothing to verify against. Needs Location.latitude/longitude/geofenceRadiusM (plus a settings surface to set them, and a defined out-of-range policy: block vs warn vs record-and-flag). No agent caught this schema hole.

missed_gaps.title: Manager has no way to author the shift notes the employee app displays
missed_gaps.detail: Employee Shift Detail renders 'Note from your manager' (EmployeeApp.jsx lines 141-148) and the Shift entity carries notes ('Bring your own knife kit.'), but AssignShiftDialog.jsx — the only shift create/edit surface — has no notes field at all (only employee select + start/end time). The components agent noted a Textarea primitive 'will be needed on the manager side too' but no agent flagged the missing notes input in the assign dialog itself, so as designed the field the employee app depends on can never be populated.

missed_gaps.title: Overtime conflict type has no supporting configuration or data anywhere
missed_gaps.detail: readme.md and ConflictChip's documented semantics list three conflict kinds: double-booked, outside availability, and overtime. Double-booking and availability have data sources in the model, but overtime detection requires a weekly-hours threshold (e.g. 40 hrs) and rules (per location? per employee? daily OT?), and no field, entity, settings screen, or gap entry from any of the six agents covers it. Before conflict detection can honor the readme's promise, an OT threshold must be added to Location (or org settings) with a management surface — or overtime must be explicitly cut from v1 conflict detection.

missed_gaps.title: Cross-kit mock data is mutually inconsistent and unusable as seed data
missed_gaps.detail: Nobody cross-checked the two kits' demo data: the manager grid week is Mon Jul 6 – Sun Jul 12, 2026 (Jul 12 IS a Sunday), but the employee kit and SwapApprovals both say 'Sat Jul 12' and 'Sun Jul 13' (EmployeeApp.jsx lines 12-13, SwapApprovals.jsx lines 6-7) — a day-of-week error inconsistent with the schedule grid. Priya Shah's swap request describes a 'Sat Jul 12, 4–10 PM' Server shift, but her only Saturday shift in INITIAL_SHIFTS is 11:00 AM–4:00 PM on Sat 11. And the unexamined uploads/pasted-1783291965045-0.png (the only user-provided visual reference, a toast mockup) reads 'Schedule published / 12 employees notified' — a THIRD notified count alongside the confirm dialog's 5 and the toast's 10. Consequence for the port: the mock arrays cannot be translated into a coherent Prisma seed without reconciliation; a consistent seed dataset must be authored fresh.

corrections: managerKit overstates a Sidebar data need: it lists 'Aggregate conflict count for schedule badge' as data the sidebar needs, but Sidebar.jsx renders nav items as icon+label only — there is no badge or count anywhere in the sidebar. The conflict count appears only in the Schedule header ConflictChip (ManagerApp.jsx line 167, fed by the computed conflictCount at line 102) and as hardcoded text on the dashboard card.

dataModel misattributes evidence for Notification.tone: it claims tone is 'UI Badge tone on Notifications screen', but NotificationsScreen (EmployeeApp.jsx lines 354-371) renders only title, body, and relative time — no Badge and no use of tone at all. The employeeKit agent's version ('tone field is never rendered') is the correct one; the field is mock-data-only and its inclusion in the schema should be treated as speculative, not screen-evidenced.

infra's icon inventory and porting plan ('the needed set is ~18 icons') is incomplete: it was sourced from the guidelines/iconography.html specimen list plus literal grep, which misses the dynamic data-lucide={...} usages. Actually-used icons absent from their list: 'layout-dashboard' (manager Sidebar Dashboard nav, Sidebar.jsx line 3), 'calendar-check' (manager Availability nav AND employee Availability tab, Sidebar.jsx line 6 / EmployeeApp.jsx line 449), and 'timer' (employee Clock tab, EmployeeApp.jsx line 450). Conversely 'settings', 'home', 'plus', 'check', 'x', 'alert-triangle', 'phone', and 'search' appear only in the specimen card, not in any app screen.

employeeKit's minor gap 'edits are lost when switching Simple/Advanced tabs' is wrong: the avail/hours state lives in AvailabilityScreen (lines 162-166), which stays mounted across the Simple/Advanced Tabs toggle — switching modes preserves all edits. State is only lost when navigating away via the bottom tab bar (which unmounts AvailabilityScreen), which the same entry does also state correctly. Only the second half of the claim should survive synthesis.

dataModel's clock-in endpoint description ('server verifies geolocation against Location') is internally inconsistent with its own Location entity, which has no latitude/longitude/geofence-radius fields — verification as described is impossible against the proposed schema (filed as a missed gap with the fix).

dataModel's EmployeeProfile.positionId as a single required FK contradicts the export's seed evidence of employees working multiple positions (Sam Torres: Server + Dishwasher shifts; Alex Kim: Host shifts + Dishwasher shift and Dishwasher open-shift claim) — the 'primary role' framing undersells that a qualification join model is required for eligibility filtering, 'anyone qualified' swaps, and conflict detection (filed as a missed gap).

Verified accurate under spot-check (no correction needed, synthesis can rely on these): dead time-off Approve/Deny (ManagerApp.jsx 219-220), dead 'Add shift' (line 171), publish 5-vs-10 copy mismatch (lines 256 vs 150), DashboardScreen ignoring its conflictCount prop in favor of hardcoded '2 shifts have conflicts' (lines 15, 37), ANCHOR = new Date(2026, 6, 6) with Today→offset 0 (ScheduleView.jsx 6, 180), SwapApprovals' shared decide() for both Approve and Deny (lines 13-15, 42-43), AvailabilityOverview showing 5 of 10 employees as weekday booleans, dead employee 'Request swap' buttons (EmployeeApp.jsx 149, 346), local-only Claim (line 329), 'Send request' that only closes the dialog (line 253), hardcoded 'Confirmed' badges (lines 108, 126), Button dropping style/hardcoding #fff (Button.jsx 48-50), Toast's checkmark glyph for all tones (Toast.jsx 42-44, only the color varies), Select's unicode ▾ caret (Select.jsx 47), --shadow-focus defined in tokens but unused by Button, no index.js barrel anywhere, and zero dark-mode/prefers-color-scheme hits.
critic.summary: Coverage was strong on all load-bearing JSX, tokens, and infra files; 32 files went unread — 15 of 16 component .prompt.md files (usage intent, e.g. ShiftBlock status→color semantics), 12 of 14 guidelines specimen cards (mostly token-redundant, though effects-motion.html demonstrates the CSS :hover/:active pattern the porting plan recommends and colors-semantic.html maps statuses to product meanings), 4 *.card.html preview shells, and — most notably — uploads/pasted-1783291965045-0.png, the only user-provided visual reference, a toast mockup reading '12 employees notified' (a third inconsistent count vs the dialog's 5 and toast's 10). Spot-checks confirmed essentially all headline claims (dead buttons, hardcoded stats, ANCHOR date, shared decide(), 5-vs-10 copy bug); corrections are mostly evidence-attribution errors: a fabricated sidebar conflict badge, dataModel citing a Badge tone the Notifications screen never renders, an icon inventory missing layout-dashboard/calendar-check/timer, and a false claim that Simple/Advanced tab switching loses availability edits. The biggest misses across all six agents: the demo data proves employees work multiple positions while the data model allows one (no qualification model, breaking 'anyone qualified' swaps and eligibility filtering); 'coverage gaps' is uncomputable without an undesigned staffing-requirements concept; iOS web push requires an installed PWA + service worker that no one planned for; Location lacks the coordinates/geofence radius its own clock-in verification requires; the manager UI has no input for the shift notes employees see; overtime conflicts have no threshold config anywhere; and the cross-kit mock data is internally contradictory (wrong day-of-week for Jul 12-13, Priya's swap referencing a nonexistent shift), so a Prisma seed must be authored fresh rather than translated.
